# UC-21.4.5 · Port Container Crane Cycle Time Analytics

## Description

Crane cycle time is a key productivity metric for port operations. Tracking enables operator performance comparison and equipment optimization.

## Value

Service and risk leaders use signals from Port Container Crane Cycle Time Analytics to move crews, budget, and customer or regulator communications while the situation is still controllable, so the organization steers from live data rather than a post-incident report.

## Implementation

Capture crane PLM (Position Location Measurement) data. Calculate gross and net crane rates. Compare operators and shifts. Identify delays from vessel configuration and weather. **Domain context:** Port productivity is often measured in moves per hour (MPH) per crane and ship working rate—weather and twin lifts materially affect cycle time. **Splunk:** `moves_per_hour` in the sample equals `count` per bin—rename to `moves_in_bin` unless you normalize to hourly rate explicitly.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Terminal operating system / crane PLC via HEC.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=logistics` `sourcetype="crane:cycle"` fields `crane_id`, `cycle_time_sec`, `move_type`, `operator_id`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Capture crane PLM (Position Location Measurement) data. Calculate gross and net crane rates. Compare operators and shifts. Identify delays from vessel configuration and weather. **Domain context:** Port productivity is often measured in moves per hour (MPH) per crane and ship working rate—weather and twin lifts materially affect cycle time. **Splunk:** `moves_per_hour` in the sample equals `count` per bin—rename to `moves_in_bin` unless you normalize to hourly rate explicitly.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=logistics sourcetype="crane:cycle"
| bin _time span=1h
| stats avg(cycle_time_sec) as avg_cycle, perc95(cycle_time_sec) as p95_cycle, count as moves by crane_id, _time
| eval moves_per_hour=moves
| table _time, crane_id, avg_cycle, p95_cycle, moves_per_hour
```

Understanding this SPL

**Port Container Crane Cycle Time Analytics** — Crane cycle time is a key productivity metric for port operations. Tracking enables operator performance comparison and equipment optimization.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=logistics` `sourcetype="crane:cycle"` fields `crane_id`, `cycle_time_sec`, `move_type`, `operator_id`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Terminal operating system / crane PLC via HEC. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: logistics; **sourcetype**: crane:cycle. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=logistics, sourcetype="crane:cycle". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Discretizes time or numeric ranges with `bin`/`bucket`.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by crane_id, _time** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **moves_per_hour** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Pipeline stage (see **Port Container Crane Cycle Time Analytics**): table _time, crane_id, avg_cycle, p95_cycle, moves_per_hour


Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the fleet telematics or TMS portal and, for warehouse or port flows, the WMS or terminal operations screen for the same vehicle, container, or order window. Align GPS time zones and odometer units with the source system.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Line chart (cycle time trend), Bar chart (MPH by crane), Table (shift performance).

## SPL

```spl
index=logistics sourcetype="crane:cycle"
| bin _time span=1h
| stats avg(cycle_time_sec) as avg_cycle, perc95(cycle_time_sec) as p95_cycle, count as moves by crane_id, _time
| eval moves_per_hour=moves
| table _time, crane_id, avg_cycle, p95_cycle, moves_per_hour
```

## Visualization

Line chart (cycle time trend), Bar chart (MPH by crane), Table (shift performance).
